humanities_role_explanation: Evaluates the capability of multimodal models to understand domain-specific Christian iconography and identify Christian saints in religious art.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Evaluates multimodal models using artwork images (paintings) alongside textual prompts featuring either class labels (saint names) or detailed semantic descriptions from the Iconclass classification system.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Performs single-label iconographical image classification to identify 10 distinct categories of Christian saints under zero-shot conditions (using names vs. detailed descriptions) and few-shot learning conditions.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: The evaluation framework is specifically focused on Christian iconography in art history rather than general-purpose tasks.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates OpenAI's GPT-4o and GPT-4o-mini, as well as Google's Gemini 2.5 Pro and Gemini 2.5 Flash.

llm_is_primary_system.note: Multimodal LLMs (and VLMs) are the primary systems being evaluated on their zero-shot and few-shot capabilities.

arts_humanities_data.note: Focuses heavily on Visual Arts and Performing Arts and Religious Studies through datasets of paintings depicting Christian iconography.

quantitative_metrics.note: Evaluates model performance quantitatively using top-1 accuracy scores.

published_after_march_2023.note: Published as a preprint in September 2025.

english_language.note: Both the paper and the prompt datasets are in English.

summary: This study benchmarks the performance of Vision-Language Models (CLIP, SigLIP) and Multimodal Large Language Models (GPT-4o, Gemini 2.5) on classifying Christian iconography. Using curated subsets of three cultural heritage datasets (ArtDL, ICONCLASS, and Wikidata) representing the ten most common saints, the authors test models under zero-shot (labels only), description-enriched, and few-shot configurations. The results reveal that advanced multimodal LLMs can outperform traditional fine-tuned supervised baselines, though performance remains sensitive to data consistency and formatting.
key_findings: Gemini 2.5 Pro and GPT-4o achieved high accuracies on ArtDL (90.45% and 88.20% respectively) and ICONCLASS (84.82% and 75.43% respectively), outperforming a fine-tuned ResNet50 baseline. Conversely, the Wikidata dataset proved challenging for LLMs, with the contrastive model SigLIP-SO400M-Patch14-384 reaching the top accuracy of 66.29%. Enriching prompts with Iconclass descriptions generally improved zero-shot performance, whereas few-shot learning with five exemplars typically resulted in lower accuracies.
